heading: Awarding of costs and certain fees
label: 7430 Awarding of costs and certain fees
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title
Prior ProvisionsA prior section 7430 was renumbered .section 7437 of this title

Amendments2019— substituted “Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals” for “Internal Revenue Service Office of Appeals” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 116–252018—Subsec. (c)(6). substituted “United States Court of Federal Claims” for “United States Claims Court”.Pub. L. 115–1412017—Subsec. (c)(1). substituted “for ‘calendar year 2016’ in subparagraph (A)(ii)” for “for ‘calendar year 1992’ in subparagraph (B)” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 115–972000—Subsec. (c)(3). , substituted “Attorneys’ ” for “Attorneys” in heading.Pub. L. 106–554, § 1(a)(7) [title III, § 319(25)(A)]Subsec. (c)(3)(B). , substituted “attorneys’ fees” for “attorneys fees” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 106–554, § 1(a)(7) [title III, § 319(25)(B)]1998—Subsec. (b)(4), (5). , redesignated par. (5) as (4).Pub. L. 105–206, § 6012(h)Subsec. (c)(1)(B)(iii). , substituted “$125” for “$110” and inserted “the difficulty of the issues presented in the case, or the local availability of tax expertise,” before “justifies a higher rate”.Pub. L. 105–206, § 3101(a)Subsec. (c)(2). , added concluding provisions and struck out former concluding provisions which read as follows: “Such term shall only include costs incurred on or after the earlier of (i) the date of the receipt by the taxpayer of the notice of the decision of the Internal Revenue Service Office of Appeals, or (ii) the date of the notice of deficiency.”Pub. L. 105–206, § 3101(b)Subsec. (c)(3). , substituted “attorneys” for “attorney’s” in heading and amended text of par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of paragraphs (1) and (2), fees for the services of an individual (whether or not an attorney) who is authorized to practice before the Tax Court or before the Internal Revenue Service shall be treated as fees for the services of an attorney.”Pub. L. 105–206, § 3101(c)Subsec. (c)(4)(B)(iii), (iv). , added cl. (iii) and redesignated former cl. (iii) as (iv).Pub. L. 105–206, § 3101(d)Subsec. (c)(4)(D). , substituted “subparagraph (A)(ii)” for “subparagraph (A)(iii)” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–206, § 6014(e)Subsec. (c)(4)(E). , added subpar. (E).Pub. L. 105–206, § 3101(e)(1)Subsec. (g). , added subsec. (g).Pub. L. 105–206, § 3101(e)(2)1997—Subsec. (b)(5). , added par. (5).Pub. L. 105–34, § 1285(b)Subsec. (c)(4)(D). , added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 105–34, § 1453(a)Subsec. (f)(2). , substituted “the filing of a petition for review with” for “appeal to” and inserted at end “If the Secretary sends by certified or registered mail a notice of such decision to the petitioner, no proceeding in the Tax Court may be initiated under this paragraph unless such petition is filed before the 91st day after the date of such mailing.”Pub. L. 105–34, § 1285(c)Subsec. (f)(3). , added par. (3).Pub. L. 105–34, § 1285(a)1996—Subsec. (b)(1). , inserted at end “Any failure to agree to an extension of the time for the assessment of any tax shall not be taken into account for purposes of determining whether the prevailing party meets the requirements of the preceding sentence.”Pub. L. 104–168, § 703(a)Subsec. (b)(3), (4). , redesignated par. (4) as (3) and struck out former par. (3) which read as follows:Pub. L. 104–168, § 704(a)“(3)Exclusion of declaratory judgment proceedings.—“(A) .—No award for reasonable litigation costs may be made under subsection (a) with respect to any declaratory judgment proceeding.In general“(B) c.—Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any proceeding which involves the revocation of a determination that the organization is described in section 501(c)(3).”Exception for section 501()(3) determination revocation proceedingsSubsec. (c)(1). , inserted closing provisions “In the case of any calendar year beginning after 1996, the dollar amount referred to in clause (iii) shall be increased by an amount equal to such dollar amount multiplied by the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for such calendar year, by substituting ‘calendar year 1995’ for ‘calendar year 1992’ in subparagraph (B) thereof. If any dollar amount after being increased under the preceding sentence is not a multiple of $10, such dollar amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of $10.”Pub. L. 104–168, § 702(a)(3)Subsec. (c)(1)(B)(iii). , (2), substituted “$110” for “$75” and struck out “an increase in the cost of living or” before “a special factor,”.Pub. L. 104–168, § 702(a)(1)Subsec. (c)(2)(B). , substituted “paragraph (4)(C)” for “paragraph (4)(B)”.Pub. L. 104–168, § 701(c)(1)Subsec. (c)(4)(A). , redesignated cls. (ii) and (iii) as (i) and (ii), respectively, and struck out former cl. (i) which read as follows: “which establishes that the position of the United States in the proceeding was not substantially justified,”.Pub. L. 104–168, § 701(a)Subsec. (c)(4)(B). , added subpar. (B). Former subpar. (B) redesignated (C).Pub. L. 104–168, § 701(b)Subsec. (c)(4)(C). , substituted “Any determination under this paragraph” for “Any determination under subparagraph (A)”.Pub. L. 104–168, § 701(c)(2), redesignated subpar. (B) as (C).Pub. L. 104–168, § 701(b)1988—, substituted “costs” for “court costs” in section catchline and amended text generally, revising and restating provisions so as to include costs and fees in administrative proceedings.Pub. L. 100–647, § 6239(a)Subsec. (c)(2)(A)(iii). , amended cl. (iii) generally. Prior to amendment, cl. (iii) read as follows: “meets the requirements of , United States Code (as in effect on the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1986 and applied by taking into account the commencement of the proceeding described in subsection (a) in lieu of the initiation of the adjudication referred to in such section).”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1015(i)section 504(b)(1)(B) of title 51986—Subsec. (a). , inserted “(payable in the case of the Tax Court in the same manner as such an award by a district court)” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1551(f)Subsec. (b). , (b), redesignated pars. (2) to (4) as (1) to (3), respectively, added par. (4), and struck out former par. (1), maximum dollar amount, which read as follows: “The amount of reasonable litigation costs which may be awarded under subsection (a) with respect to any prevailing party in any civil proceeding shall not exceed $25,000.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1551(a)Subsec. (c)(1)(A). , amended subpar. (A) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (A) read as follows: “The term ‘reasonable litigation costs’ includes—Pub. L. 99–514, § 1551(c)“(i) reasonable court costs,“(ii) the reasonable expenses of expert witnesses in connection with the civil proceeding,“(iii) the reasonable cost of any study, analysis, engineering report, test, or project which is found by the court to be necessary for the preparation of the party’s case, and“(iv) reasonable fees paid or incurred for the services of attorneys in connection with the civil proceeding.”Subsec. (c)(2)(A). , substituted “was not substantially justified” for “was unreasonable” in cl. (i), and added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1551(d)Subsec. (c)(4). , added par. (4).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1551(e)Subsec. (f). , struck out subsec. (f), termination, which read as follows: “This section shall not apply to any proceeding commenced after .”Pub. children.text: reasonable administrative costs incurred in connection with such administrative proceeding within the Internal Revenue Service, and
reasonable litigation costs incurred in connection with such court proceeding.

children.heading: Requirement that administrative remedies be exhausted
children.label: 1 Requirement that administrative remedies be exhausted
children.text: A judgment for reasonable litigation costs shall not be awarded under subsection (a) in any court proceeding unless the court determines that the prevailing party has exhausted the administrative remedies available to such party within the Internal Revenue Service. Any failure to agree to an extension of the time for the assessment of any tax shall not be taken into account for purposes of determining whether the prevailing party meets the requirements of the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Only costs allocable to the United States
children.label: 2 Only costs allocable to the United States
children.text: An award under subsection (a) shall be made only for reasonable litigation and administrative costs which are allocable to the United States and not to any other party.

children.heading: Costs denied where party prevailing protracts proceedings
children.label: 3 Costs denied where party prevailing protracts proceedings
children.text: No award for reasonable litigation and administrative costs may be made under subsection (a) with respect to any portion of the administrative or court proceeding during which the prevailing party has unreasonably protracted such proceeding.

children.heading: Period for applying to IRS for administrative costs
children.label: 4 Period for applying to IRS for administrative costs
children.text: An award may be made under subsection (a) by the Internal Revenue Service for reasonable administrative costs only if the prevailing party files an application with the Internal Revenue Service for such costs before the 91st day after the date on which the final decision of the Internal Revenue Service as to the determination of the tax, interest, or penalty is mailed to such party.

children.label: A
children.text: reasonable court costs, and

children.label: i
children.text: the reasonable expenses of expert witnesses in connection with a court proceeding, except that no expert witness shall be compensated at a rate in excess of the highest rate of compensation for expert witnesses paid by the United States,

children.label: ii
children.text: the reasonable cost of any study, analysis, engineering report, test, or project which is found by the court to be necessary for the preparation of the party’s case, and

children.label: iii
children.text: reasonable fees paid or incurred for the services of attorneys in connection with the court proceeding, except that such fees shall not be in excess of $125 per hour unless the court determines that a special factor, such as the limited availability of qualified attorneys for such proceeding, the difficulty of the issues presented in the case, or the local availability of tax expertise, justifies a higher rate.

children.label: A
children.text: any administrative fees or similar charges imposed by the Internal Revenue Service, and

children.label: B
children.text: expenses, costs, and fees described in paragraph (1)(B), except that any determination made by the court under clause (ii) or (iii) thereof shall be made by the Internal Revenue Service in cases where the determination under paragraph (4)(C) of the awarding of reasonable administrative costs is made by the Internal Revenue Service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraphs (1) and (2), fees for the services of an individual (whether or not an attorney) who is authorized to practice before the Tax Court or before the Internal Revenue Service shall be treated as fees for the services of an attorney.

children.heading: Pro bono services
children.label: B Pro bono services
children.text: The court may award reasonable attorneys’ fees under subsection (a) in excess of the attorneys’ fees paid or incurred if such fees are less than the reasonable attorneys’ fees because an individual is representing the prevailing party for no fee or for a fee which (taking into account all the facts and circumstances) is no more than a nominal fee. This subparagraph shall apply only if such award is paid to such individual or such individual’s employer.

children.label: I
children.text: has substantially prevailed with respect to the amount in controversy, or

children.label: II
children.text: has substantially prevailed with respect to the most significant issue or set of issues presented, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which meets the requirements of the 1st sentence of , United States Code (as in effect on ) except to the extent differing procedures are established by rule of court and meets the requirements of section 2412(d)(2)(B) of such title 28 (as so in effect).section 2412(d)(1)(B) of title 28October 22, 1986

children.heading: Exception if United States establishes that its position was substantially justified
children.label: B Exception if United States establishes that its position was substantially justified

children.heading: General rule
children.label: i General rule
children.text: A party shall not be treated as the prevailing party in a proceeding to which subsection (a) applies if the United States establishes that the position of the United States in the proceeding was substantially justified.

children.heading: Presumption of no justification if Internal Revenue Service did not follow certain published guidance
children.label: ii Presumption of no justification if Internal Revenue Service did not follow certain published guidance
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the position of the United States shall be presumed not to be substantially justified if the Internal Revenue Service did not follow its applicable published guidance in the administrative proceeding. Such presumption may be rebutted.

children.heading: Effect of losing on substantially similar issues
children.label: iii Effect of losing on substantially similar issues
children.text: In determining for purposes of clause (i) whether the position of the United States was substantially justified, the court shall take into account whether the United States has lost in courts of appeal for other circuits on substantially similar issues.

children.label: I
children.text: regulations, revenue rulings, revenue procedures, information releases, notices, and announcements, and

children.label: II
children.text: any of the following which are issued to the taxpayer: private letter rulings, technical advice memoranda, and determination letters.

children.heading: Determination as to prevailing party
children.label: C Determination as to prevailing party
i
children.text: in the case where the final determination with respect to the tax, interest, or penalty is made at the administrative level, by the Internal Revenue Service, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case where such final determination is made by a court, the court.

children.heading: Special rules for applying net worth requirement
children.label: D Special rules for applying net worth requirement
I
children.text: an estate but shall be determined as of the date of the decedent’s death, and

children.label: II
children.text: a trust but shall be determined as of the last day of the taxable year involved in the proceeding, and

children.label: ii
children.text: individuals filing a joint return shall be treated as separate individuals for purposes of clause (i) of such section.

children.heading: Special rules where judgment less than taxpayer’s offer
children.label: E Special rules where judgment less than taxpayer’s offer

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A party to a court proceeding meeting the requirements of subparagraph (A)(ii) shall be treated as the prevailing party if the liability of the taxpayer pursuant to the judgment in the proceeding (determined without regard to interest) is equal to or less than the liability of the taxpayer which would have been so determined if the United States had accepted a qualified offer of the party under subsection (g).

children.label: I
children.text: any judgment issued pursuant to a settlement; or

children.label: II
children.text: any proceeding in which the amount of tax liability is not in issue, including any declaratory judgment proceeding, any proceeding to enforce or quash any summons issued pursuant to this title, and any action to restrain disclosure under section 6110(f).

children.label: I
children.text: the determination under clause (i) shall be made by reference to the last qualified offer made with respect to the tax liability at issue in the proceeding; and

children.label: II
children.text: reasonable administrative and litigation costs shall only include costs incurred on and after the date of such offer.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: iv Coordination
children.text: This subparagraph shall not apply to a party which is a prevailing party under any other provision of this paragraph.

children.heading: Administrative proceedings
children.label: 5 Administrative proceedings
children.text: The term “administrative proceeding” means any procedure or other action before the Internal Revenue Service.

children.heading: Court proceedings
children.label: 6 Court proceedings
children.text: The term “court proceeding” means any civil action brought in a court of the United States (including the Tax Court and the United States Court of Federal Claims).

children.label: A
children.text: the position taken by the United States in a judicial proceeding to which subsection (a) applies, and

children.label: i
children.text: the date of the receipt by the taxpayer of the notice of the decision of the Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of the notice of deficiency.

children.heading: Special rules for payment of costs
children.label: d Special rules for payment of costs

children.heading: Reasonable administrative costs
children.label: 1 Reasonable administrative costs
children.text: An award for reasonable administrative costs shall be payable out of funds appropriated under , United States Code.section 1304 of title 31

children.heading: Reasonable litigation costs
children.label: 2 Reasonable litigation costs
children.text: An award for reasonable litigation costs shall be payable in the case of the Tax Court in the same manner as such an award by a district court.
multiple actions which could have been joined or consolidated, or
a case or cases involving a return or returns of the same taxpayer (including joint returns of married individuals) which could have been joined in a single court proceeding in the same court,

children.heading: Court proceedings
children.label: 1 Court proceedings
children.text: An order granting or denying (in whole or in part) an award for reasonable litigation or administrative costs under subsection (a) in a court proceeding, may be incorporated as a part of the decision or judgment in the court proceeding and shall be subject to appeal in the same manner as the decision or judgment.

children.heading: Administrative proceedings
children.label: 2 Administrative proceedings
children.text: A decision granting or denying (in whole or in part) an award for reasonable administrative costs under subsection (a) by the Internal Revenue Service shall be subject to the filing of a petition for review with the Tax Court under rules similar to the rules under section 7463 (without regard to the amount in dispute). If the Secretary sends by certified or registered mail a notice of such decision to the petitioner, no proceeding in the Tax Court may be initiated under this paragraph unless such petition is filed before the 91st day after the date of such mailing.

children.heading: Appeal of Tax Court decision
children.label: 3 Appeal of Tax Court decision
children.text: An order of the Tax Court disposing of a petition under paragraph (2) shall be reviewable in the same manner as a decision of the Tax Court, but only with respect to the matters determined in such order.

children.label: A
children.text: is made by the taxpayer to the United States during the qualified offer period;

children.label: B
children.text: specifies the offered amount of the taxpayer’s liability (determined without regard to interest);

children.label: C
children.text: is designated at the time it is made as a qualified offer for purposes of this section; and

children.label: D
children.text: remains open during the period beginning on the date it is made and ending on the earliest of the date the offer is rejected, the date the trial begins, or the 90th day after the date the offer is made.

children.label: A
children.text: beginning on the date on which the first letter of proposed deficiency which allows the taxpayer an opportunity for administrative review in the Internal Revenue Service Independent Office of Appeals is sent, and

children.label: B
children.text: ending on the date which is 30 days before the date the case is first set for trial.
